function.name: creel_search
function.description: Search for relevant content chunks across one or more Creel topics using semantic similarity. Use this when you need to find information related to a query. Returns ranked chunks with scores, document citations, and optionally linked content.

topic_ids.description: List of topic IDs to search within. You must specify at least one topic.

query_text.description: The natural language query to search for. This will be embedded and compared against stored chunks.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

follow_links.description: If true, also return chunks linked to the top results. Useful for discovering related content.

link_depth.description: How many hops to follow when follow_links is true. Higher values find more distant connections.

function.name: creel_get_context
function.description: Retrieve temporal context from a specific document. Returns chunks in chronological order, optionally filtered by recency. Use this when you already know which document to read and want its recent content or a time-bounded slice.

document_id.description: The ID of the document to retrieve context from.

last_n.description: Return only the last N chunks from the document. Useful for getting the most recent entries.

since.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Only return chunks created after this time. Example: 2026-01-15T00:00:00Z

function.name: creel_add_memory
function.description: Queue a memory observation for processing. Returns a job_id; the memory maintenance worker handles deduplication. Memories persist across sessions and are scoped to the calling principal and a named scope. Optionally structure the memory as a subject/predicate/object triple for precise retrieval.

scope.description: The memory scope (namespace). Use a consistent scope name to group related memories, such as 'user_preferences' or 'project_notes'.

content.description: The memory content as natural language text. This is the primary content that will be stored and searchable.

subject.description: Optional subject of a structured triple. Example: 'Nick'

predicate.description: Optional predicate of a structured triple. Example: 'prefers'

object.description: Optional object of a structured triple. Example: 'dark mode'

function.name: creel_get_memories
function.description: Retrieve all stored memories for the calling principal. Optionally filter to one or more scopes. Use this to recall previously stored observations, facts, or preferences. If no scopes are specified, returns memories across all scopes.

scopes.description: Optional list of scope names to filter by. If empty or omitted, returns memories from all scopes.

function.name: creel_add_messages
function.description: Send conversation messages for automatic fact extraction. Creel will analyze the messages and extract relevant facts as memories. Returns job IDs for the extraction jobs.

scope.description: The memory scope to store extracted facts in.

role.description: The role of the message sender (e.g. 'user', 'assistant').

messages.description: List of conversation messages to extract facts from.

function.name: creel_list_memories
function.description: List all memories in a given scope. Use this to see everything stored in a scope without searching. Optionally include memories that have been invalidated.

scope.description: The memory scope to list memories from.

include_invalidated.description: If true, also return memories that have been marked as invalidated. Defaults to false.

function.name: creel_delete_memory
function.description: Permanently delete a stored memory by its ID. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

id.description: The ID of the memory to delete.

function.name: creel_upload_document
function.description: Upload text content as a new document in a topic. The content will be chunked, embedded, and indexed for future search. Use this to store new information that should be retrievable later.

topic_id.description: The ID of the topic to upload the document into.

name.description: A human-readable name for the document.

content.description: The text content to upload. This will be chunked and embedded for semantic search.

slug.description: Optional URL-friendly identifier. If omitted, one will be generated from the name.

content_type.description: MIME type of the content. Defaults to text/plain. Use text/markdown for Markdown content.

function.name: creel_list_topics
function.description: List all topics you have access to. Topics are collections of documents organized by subject area. Use this to discover what knowledge bases are available before searching.

function.name: creel_get_document
function.description: Retrieve metadata for a specific document by its ID. Returns the document name, type, topic, status, and other metadata. Does not return the document content; use creel_get_context for that.

id.description: The ID of the document to retrieve.

function.name: creel_list_documents
function.description: List all documents within a topic. Use this to see what documents are available in a topic before searching or retrieving context.

topic_id.description: The ID of the topic to list documents from.
